reasoning: Spain have already reached the final and are odds-on favourites after a dominant 2-0 win over France, with ESPN showing them at -156 to lift the trophy and riding a 37-game unbeaten streak that ties the longest in international football history. Their midfield control and tournament-best defence have carried them through comfortably, and they now await the winner of England vs Argentina, whom bookmakers price as roughly even with England the slimmest of favourites (around -125). I lean England as the likelier finalist and runner-up given their more well-rounded, resilient squad, Kane and Bellingham both on six goals, and Argentina's notably soft route plus a defence exposed at the fullbacks; several ESPN writers also see Messi's side running out of gas. Argentina remain the live dark horse: a 39-year-old Messi in the form of his life (a tournament-leading eight goals) plus Emiliano Martinez's penalty pedigree make them lethal in a one-off knockout.
